Economic spine: kura(E0) → reclaimed shinden + repaired kura(E1) → granary + 2 workshops(E2) → counting-house + sake/silk sub-economy + branch holding(E3) → meibutsu workshop quarter + multi-valley holdings(E4) → famous-product quarter surpassing the original(E5). Martial spine: rusty door-bar + lone Jūbei(E0) → cleared drill yard + a gate night-watch(E1) → 2-3 men-at-arms on rota + low palisade(E2) → roofed dōjō + barracks + small standing squad(E3) → wall+gatehouse + armoury + watchtower + road-patrol that secures trade (combat-earned Influence made visible)(E4) → formal retainer barracks + training-school(E5). The two fabrics are SYSTEMICALLY linked: the standing patrol (martial) is what makes the branch holdings and trade routes (economic) safe enough to exist, so defences are not decoration — clearing bandits and garrisoning roads is literally what lets the next economic node be built, fusing combat and economy as the design briefs demand.

key_designs.name: The growing ROSTER — named representative members joining per stage, as cards
key_designs.description: The roster is the felt reward. ~5-8 NAMED members join per stage, each a small card (name, role, one-line arc hook, a production OR garrison contribution, and a faction-source tag: estate-hired / village-seconded / origin-recruited). E0: just the household + Jūbei (drillmaster) + bottom-rung peers Sota & Mago. E1 adds: green recruit Tokujirō (the mirror who later gets taught), a first day-labour gang. E2 adds the first paid outsiders: men-at-arms Gohei & Yataro (ex-ashigaru hired off the road), maidservant-expansion under Oai, carpenter-apprentice (via village Carpenter Risuke), a charcoal artisan. E3 adds: a seconded village artisan team (Weaver Onatsu's apprentices), a patrol-leader NPC, a sake-brewer partnership (Tokuemon's stake), a counting-house clerk under Tanomo, kitchen + guest staff. E4 adds: a standing squad of named men-at-arms (a kumigashira/patrol-corporal, 2-3 named soldiers), origin-recruited porters/guards (Kanta + guild mates), branch-holding stewards. E5 adds: the protagonist's OWN students (Tokujirō now teaches under him; famine-orphan recruits 'started from zero'), a master artisan for the meibutsu product, and the Origin FATHER as a late trade/logistics contributor (see ties-to-vision). Each card's contribution is data-driven and slots into the existing idle-producer / garrison systems.

key_designs.name: Estate growth is a buildable spend-loop wired to the universal reveal/Influence bus
key_designs.description: Each holding/building/defence/recruit is a registry entry with: a cost (koku/coin/materials/labour-time + sometimes a martial prerequisite like 'roads cleared of bandits'), an unlock predicate (Influence band + rank floor + story flags), a diegetic reveal line, and an Influence-on-recognition payoff (the build only converts to Influence when the relevant authority acknowledges it — a recorded granary census, a domain noting the secured road). This keeps estate growth diegetic ('the books now show a granary, a drill hall, and eleven on the roster'), deterministic (costs/payoffs are data, RNG-free), and headlessly testable (force an Influence band, assert the right buildings/roster cards are revealed and that no build was free). Buildings can be upgraded fractally (drill yard → roofed dōjō → school), and a small scripted set of dents (a fire, a debt called, a raid that the garrison must repel) can damage a holding — small, recoverable, never a wipe — giving the estate visible tension as well as growth.

facets.ties_to_vision: This facet IS the §1.2 pillar-1 spine ('the estate-rise is a perseverance fantasy at grand scale') and pillar-2 ('the interface is the reward') made physically tangible: the estate's silhouette is the progress bar, and each new building/roster card is a diegetic UI reveal, not silent menu growth. It honours pillar-5 (no reset — the estate persists and accretes across all six stages and five tiers) and pillar-7 (fractal incrementality — each building arrives minimal and itself unlocks). It directly executes the human's redesign §2 ('the estate visibly grows … declining → recovering → prosperous → grandeur-surpassed, with a growing roster') and §4 ('the canvas opens earlier' — the estate's physical growth, especially its martial buildings, is decoupled from and runs ahead of the top personal ranks). It folds in the Origin FATHER (redesign §6) cleanly: the father is added as a late E4/E5 roster card — a grounded role as an old porter/teamster or small-trade contact from Sawatari-juku who, once memory + standing reconnect the origin faction, joins the estate's expansion as a trade/logistics contributor (caravan know-how, a route, a steady supply contract), with the deliberately bittersweet edge the origin thread already carries (he and Tahei have a strained reunion; his help is earned, never a mechanical gift — access only, per the dream rule). The restored-and-surpassed E5 estate is the §1.11 endgame tableau (rebuilt omoya, the house seal, the school where the climber now teaches Tokujirō and origin friends 'from zero')."
facets.ties_to_mechanics: The estate is a pure-core data registry of HOLDINGS (each with type: land/building/defence/sub-economy/roster), each carrying an unlock predicate over GameState (Influence band + rank floor + flags), a spend cost, and an Influence-on-recognition payoff — fully deterministic and RNG-free, so balance/roster tables generate into docs/ from the same data the game runs on (single source of truth). Roster members plug into the EXISTING idle-producer and (per the combat reframe) garrison/patrol systems as assignable contributors, so the estate's growth feeds both the economy loop and the combat loop without new bespoke systems. Growth events fire through the universal rewards/unlock bus as single events (log line + panel/area reveal + perk + flag), the same bus that drives rank-ups and tier-ups, so estate growth, rank climb, and tier escalation are one coherent reveal motion. Defences/garrison are the systemic bridge to the combat pillar: securing roads/clearing bandits (combat) is a build PREREQUISITE for branch holdings and trade routes (economy), and the standing patrol's strength is a roster-derived stat — so combat materially grows the estate and the estate's barracks/armoury/dōjō materially grow combat capacity. Everything is headlessly verifiable via the DEV play API: force an Influence band + rank, assert the expected stage silhouette (buildings + roster cards) is revealed, that costs were paid, that no build granted a hidden stat, and that a scripted dent is recoverable."
risks: City-builder/4X drift (flagged in PRD §1.13): six stages of buildings/holdings/roster could slide into management tedium and dilute the cozy-labour-plus-character core. Mitigation: keep builds diegetic and story-framed (each is a beat, not a build-queue), cap the number of simultaneously-active build choices, and keep the minute-to-minute texture as labour+combat grind, not estate micromanagement.

Number-range/scope blowup across five tiers (PRD §1.13 scope guard): six estate stages × dual fabric × growing roster is a lot of content; v1 (Tiers 1-2 = stages E0-E2/early E3) must be the vertical slice, with E3-E5 as roadmap, or the facet over-scopes.

Roster bloat / cast legibility: ~5-8 named members per stage across six stages is 30-40+ NPCs on top of the existing cast — risks the same bloat the PRD warns about. Mitigation: most new roster members are LIGHT cards (role + one-line hook + contribution), reuse existing cast where possible (village artisans seconded, origin friends recruited), and only a few get full arcs.

Martial fabric vs the mediocre-start / 'no standing from combat' tension: the OLD §1.2 pillar-4 said combat is never a source of standing; the redesign reverses this, but the estate's martial buildings/garrison must still respect the humbling-start contract — the protagonist earns the barracks by service, and early defences are pathetic (a rusty bar, one old man), so the martial estate rises believably from weakness, not handed.

Lopsided-lean risk: a hard labour-vs-combat lean could leave the estate looking half-built for a long stretch (all granaries, no dōjō or vice-versa); the 'other fabric follows close behind' rule needs concrete pacing rules in the balance pass so neither lean produces an incoherent silhouette.

Decoupling tension: running estate physical growth ahead of the top ranks must not let a player skip the narrative rank gates by grinding Influence/builds — the rank floors and story flags on each holding need careful tuning so the estate leads but does not LEAP the spine.
open_questions: Exact Influence-band thresholds per stage and the koku/coin/material/labour cost curve for each building — defer to the balance facet / PRD §4, but the six-stage-to-five-tier mapping (E4 and E5 both inside T4-T5) needs the human to confirm whether E4 'Fortified Seat' is a distinct gated stage or folded into the T4 domain arc.

How literal is the estate's spatial representation? Does each stage get a redrawn estate map/visual (per capture-game-states), or is growth conveyed via panels + a roster list + a schematic? (PRD open Q: 'macro-tier spatiality — full sub-area maps vs abstract holdings boards'.)

Is the standing patrol / garrison a real assignable resource the player tunes (men-at-arms allocated to road vs gate vs branch holding), or narrative flavour with a derived strength number? This determines how deep the martial-estate management goes.
Roster member depth: how many of the ~30-40 new members get real arcs/dialogue vs being light contribution-cards? (Echoes the PRD's 'NPC roster volume for v1 vs post-launch' open question.)

The FATHER's exact grounded role and name (Origin facet owns this) — this facet assumes a late trade/logistics roster contributor with a strained-reunion hook; confirm with the Origin-facet design so the estate hook matches.

Can the player LOSE a holding permanently, or are all dents recoverable? PRD leans 'never a wipe'; confirm whether a failed raid-defence can cost a branch holding (raising combat stakes) or only damage it.

Do village-seconded artisans/allies count against village production (a real loan with a cost) when they join the estate roster? (PRD open Q on seconded-ally cost) — this affects whether roster growth is purely additive or a tradeoff.

facets.facet: Facet D — Rank↔Tier decoupling, the earlier canvas, and adding the Father to the Origin faction
facets.summary: Today's design has a single coupled spine: an 8-rung household-rank ladder where the **village** only opens at Rank 3 and the **region** opens *only* at the Rank 7 capstone ("the estate gate to the wider world"). That is exactly the "you can't leave until you're right-hand-man" trap the human flagged. The fix is to split the one spine into **three loosely-coupled progress axes** and decouple them: (1) **Household RANK** — your standing/authority *inside* the Kurosawa house (steward and martial rungs, interleaved); (2) **REACH** — a new, separate "where you may go / what you may do on the house's behalf" axis, granted as *errands and expeditions* and gated mostly by personal conditioning + a sponsor's leave, NOT by high rank; and (3) **House INFLUENCE / TIER** — the macro standing of the *house*, which still gates when each macro canvas (region→domain→Edo) becomes a real economic/holdings layer. The key reframe: **Reach runs AHEAD of Rank.** A low-mid-rank player is *sent out* as an errand-runner, escort, and expedition member long before he runs anything — he ranges the region as the *house's hands*, not as its master. Rank determines authority you wield; Reach determines ground you cover; Influence determines how big the house's footprint on that ground is. Concretely, the first regional errands fire around Rank 3–4 (mid-climb), real wilderness/region combat opens by Rank 4–5, and the *capstone now opens AUTHORITY over the region (running it), not first ACCESS to it.* I also add the missing **Father — Tahei's father Kuranosuke**, a former porter-guild man now broken/indentured by the same debt-and-quota machine, whose grounded arc (presumed-dead → found alive but ruined → redeemed by the son's standing) ties faction-3 directly into the central conspiracy and gives the origin track a male elder to complement mother Oyuki.

key_designs.name: Three decoupled axes: RANK ≠ REACH ≠ INFLUENCE (the core fix)
key_designs.description: Replace the single coupled spine with three orthogonal progress axes that advance at different rates. (1) HOUSEHOLD RANK — your title/authority inside the Kurosawa house (the interleaved labor+martial ladder from the parallel facet); gates what you may COMMAND (helpers, sub-economies, the books, retainers under you). (2) REACH — a new explicit axis = 'where you may travel and what you may do out there on the house's behalf,' expressed as discrete travel-permits/commissions (Estate grounds → Valley/Village → Near satoyama → Region roads & post-town → Domain seat → Edo-by-attendance). Reach is gated by personal CONDITIONING + a SPONSOR'S LEAVE (an NPC vouching/dispatching you), explicitly NOT by high rank. (3) HOUSE INFLUENCE/TIER — the macro standing of the house; still the gate-currency for when a macro region becomes a real holdings/economy layer the house OWNS. Rule of thumb the whole game obeys: REACH leads, RANK follows, INFLUENCE trails. You walk a road as an errand-boy (Reach) long before you have rank to command anyone on it, and long before the house has the Influence to plant holdings along it.

key_designs.name: Reach is granted as commissions/dispatches, not earned as a number to grind
key_designs.description: Reach advances through discrete, authored DISPATCH beats: a named NPC (Genemon, Jūbei, Heisuke, later Naoyuki) hands you a writ, a manifest, or a marching order that *takes you somewhere new*. Each dispatch is a one-time 'now you may go here' unlock (like the origin track's chunky milestones), gated by a low conditioning floor + a story flag (someone trusts you to not die / not embarrass the house) — never by a household promotion. This means the canvas opens via the *fiction of being useful and expendable enough to send*, which fits a mediocre nobody perfectly: you go first precisely BECAUSE you're junior and dispatchable, not despite it. The *sekisho*-checkpoint authenticity is preserved — your travel-permit IS the Reach unlock (the house's seal on a pass-document), so 'controlled roads' becomes the diegetic gate-object rather than an abstract level.

key_designs.name: Concrete early regional quests taken BEFORE high rank (mid-climb, Rank 3–5)
key_designs.description: Examples the player gets while still a trusted-hand / junior man-at-arms, NOT a steward: (a) ESCORT/PORTER — accompany Heisuke hauling the season's surplus to the down-valley market or post-town; you're muscle and a load-bearer, you SEE the region, fight off a road-side boar or a lone rice-thief, but make no decisions. (b) ERRAND/MESSAGE — carry a sealed letter to the next valley's headman; the road itself is the content (a feared ford, a 'fox-fire' ridge to misread, a bandit toll-spot). (c) EXPEDITION MEMBER — join Jūbei and two retainers clearing a boar-warren or a bandit lean-to threatening the house's outfields; you're the junior spear, you survive more than you win. (d) BOUNTY-TAG-ALONG — the house posts a small bounty on poachers in its woodlot edge; you tag along under a senior. (e) RELIEF-RUN — haul relief-rice to a flood-hit hamlet up the road (peaceful, but it's REGIONAL ground at Rank 3). Each is open-ended (a suggestion + a road you find out in the world), each earns Estate Service XP + conditioning + maybe a loot drop + a sliver of recognition — but the DECISIONS, the holdings, and the authority over that ground come much later with Rank+Influence. This is how the world opens at minute ~90 instead of hour ~15.

key_designs.name: Capstone reframed: the top rank now grants AUTHORITY over the region, not first ACCESS to it
key_designs.description: Today Rank 7's reward is literally 'the estate gate to the wider world' — i.e. the world is locked behind it. Reframe: by the time you reach the upper rungs you've ALREADY ranged the region for in-game years as the house's hands. What the capstone (and the deputy rungs before it) now unlock is the right to *direct* outward expansion: to plant a branch holding, to negotiate on the house's behalf, to command an expedition rather than march in one, to commit House Influence to a region project. So 'finishing the ladder opens the next canvas' survives in spirit — but it opens the canvas as a thing you now RUN, decoupled from when you first walked it. The macro tier-up of the HOUSE (region→domain→Edo as owned economic layers) stays gated on House Influence, exactly as locked. Personal adventuring reach simply runs years ahead of both.

key_designs.name: Mapping table: which axis gates each unlock (replaces the all-in-one rank table)
key_designs.description: A concrete remap of today's reveals across the three axes. EARLY (Rank 1–3 / Reach: estate→village→near-region / Influence: T1): labour loops, skills, village errands AND first out-of-valley escort/expedition dispatches; first humbling fight; drill yard; first loot. MID (Rank 4–6 / Reach: region roads, post-town, mountain rings / Influence: T2→early T3): you range widely as agent/escort/hunter; combat content peaks in variety; origin track opens (dream-gated memory + a travel-permit you now hold); you FIGHT for standing (clear a bandit nest = recognized Influence) before you ADMINISTER anything. LATE (Rank 7 / Reach: domain seat, Edo-by-attendance / Influence: T3→T5): you direct expeditions, plant holdings, negotiate, commit Influence to projects. The predicate on each UI reveal becomes a small boolean over (rank flag) OR (reach permit) OR (influence band) — data-driven, so the pure core can gate each panel on the *right* axis instead of forcing everything through rank.

key_designs.name: Why this stays coherent & incremental (anti-chaos guardrails)
key_designs.description: Decoupling risks the player feeling scattered, so: (1) Reach unlocks are SPARSE and authored (a handful of dispatch beats, not a grindable bar) — each is a story event, preserving 'every reveal is a plot beat.' (2) Conditioning is the shared floor — you can't accept a dangerous dispatch your body can't survive, so the mediocre-start contract still paces outward reach (no skipping ahead by sheer will). (3) Fractal incrementality holds: each newly-reached region arrives minimal (one road, one threat, one contact) and unfolds. (4) The three axes share ONE economy and feed ONE House Influence, so they read as facets of a single climb, not three games. (5) Reach NEVER grants power — only access — mirroring the locked dream-rule/no-hidden-edge discipline; what you can DO out there is still your grind-built skills and gear.

key_designs.name: ADD THE FATHER — Kuranosuke, Tahei's father (Origin faction-3)
key_designs.description: Name: KURANOSUKE (蔵之介) — a plain, period-typical commoner name echoing Tahei/Oyuki. Role: Tahei's father, ~50, a former senior porter / pack-train man of Master Denbei's transport house — the man who FIRST taught Tahei the porter's-knot (folding the existing 'Denbei taught it' note into a warmer father-son origin: Denbei the employer, Kuranosuke the actual hand who showed him; still ZERO mechanical bonus). GROUNDED ARC: not a clean reunion. He went out on the same kind of mountain consignment that later took Tahei, was injured/ruined years earlier, fell into debt-bondage to the very guild (Zenroku) over a 'lost' short-weighted load he was blamed for, and the family told the children he was 'taken on a far route' (their own small kamikakushi-shaped lie — shame dressed as absence) — mirroring the master theme. He is presumed-gone at the origin track's open (Oyuki a 'widow'); a mid-origin beat reveals he is ALIVE but broken — indentured, ashamed, hiding. GAMEPLAY FUNCTION: (1) a memory-gated origin milestone (find/free the father) that requires the player to have ranged the region (ties Reach to faction-3); (2) a hard EVIDENCE node — Kuranosuke witnessed the same short-weighting/contraband racket (Zenroku/Kuroiwa), so freeing him both reunites the family AND hands the conspiracy a witness, knitting origin into the central plot; (3) once redeemed by the son's standing, he becomes an ALLY producer — an old pack-train master who unlocks/upgrades the origin caravan/porter trade-tie (access-only: the route exists once he's free, but is still grind-built). His redemption is the origin track's emotional capstone: the son's estate achievements literally buy back the father's freedom and name — the 'they support his achievements' frame run in reverse (the achievement supports THEM).

facets.ties_to_vision: Directly serves the locked vision's core motion — 'as you rise, the world you can reach grows with you' — by making REACH an explicit, early-leading axis instead of a reward bolted onto the capstone, so the canvas genuinely opens room-by-room AND road-by-road as a continuous reveal (the signature UI-as-progression), not in one late dump. It honours the mediocre-start contract: the nobody is sent out FIRST because he is junior and dispatchable, ranging the region as the house's hands before its master — 'access, not power.' It keeps the locked tier escalation intact (House Influence still gates the macro tier-up; no reset) while removing the coupling that made the spine feel like a single locked corridor. The Father deepens the origin thread's pay-off-in-feeling-not-power principle and reinforces the 'one rotten root' theme (the same debt-and-quota machine that drowned Kuzuhara and wrote off Tahei's caravan also broke his father), tying faction-3 to the central conspiracy exactly as the cast's double-duty design intends.
facets.ties_to_mechanics: Implements cleanly in the pure-core/data-driven model: REACH becomes a third small enum/flag-set on GameState (a set of held travel-permits) alongside rank flags and the Influence band; every area/panel/quest unlock predicate becomes a boolean over (rank) OR (reach permit) OR (influence band) — replacing the current 'everything keyed off rank' table — so each reveal is gated on the correct axis and stays headlessly regression-testable via the DEV play API (assert a region escort dispatch is acceptable at Rank 3 with the right permit but NOT before conditioning floor). Dispatch/Reach unlocks are authored one-time beats fired through the existing universal rewards/unlock bus (log line + reveal + flag), so no new UI metaphor is needed. The Father slots into the existing origin 'restored ties' milestone list (chunky one-time unlocks on a guaranteed cadence) and the existing ally-producer + trade-tie systems; his evidence-node role reuses the planned investigation/evidence layer. All randomness (road encounters on dispatches) flows through the single seeded RNG. Cross-facet dependency: the interleaved labor+martial RANK ladder is owned by the parallel estate/combat facet — this facet consumes it as the 'authority' axis and must stay consistent with whatever rung list that facet lands; the combat content of early dispatches likewise hooks the combat-as-core-pillar facet.
risks: Three axes can read as scattered/confusing if surfaced as three visible bars — mitigate by keeping Reach as sparse authored dispatch beats (not a grindable meter) and by framing all three in-fiction as one climb feeding one House Influence.

Decoupling could let a player range far while staying very low rank, creating a tonal mismatch (a Rank-2 nobody wandering the domain seat) — the conditioning floor + sponsor-leave gate + sparse permits must be tuned so Reach leads rank by a believable margin, not absurdly.

Cross-facet coupling risk: this design assumes the parallel facet's longer interleaved labor/combat rank ladder; if that ladder's rung names/count change, the rank↔reach mapping table here must be re-synced (these two facets must be reconciled before §1 is re-locked).

The Father risks cast bloat and over-stuffing the conspiracy with witnesses (already has Naozane, Oharu, Zenroku) — keep his evidence role distinct (the porter/short-weighting angle specifically) so he isn't a redundant 'another guilt-sick witness.'

Making the father's rescue require regional Reach + estate standing must stay access-only — risk of implying the son's RANK magically frees him; keep it grind-built (you buy out the debt / win the petition with resources you earned), honouring the no-mechanical-gift rule.

Adding a living father slightly softens the origin track's bittersweet edge (mother already 'furious he was written off'; sweetheart may have moved on) — preserve costliness: Kuranosuke returns broken and ashamed, redemption is partial, not a clean restoration.
open_questions: Should REACH be modeled as discrete named permits (recommended: a small ordered set of travel-permits) or as a continuous 'range' number? Discrete fits the authored-beat philosophy better but the human may want finer granularity.

Exact rank↔reach offset: how many rungs ahead of rank should reach typically run (e.g. first regional dispatch at Rank 3 of ~12 interleaved rungs)? Needs a pacing pass once the parallel facet locks the rung count.

Does combat-earned standing (clearing a bandit nest while on a low-rank dispatch) feed Estate Service XP, House Influence directly, or both — and does it ever advance REACH (proving you can be trusted farther)? Recommend: yes, martial success can unlock the next Reach permit ('he came back alive AND useful').

Is the capstone-grants-authority-not-access reframe acceptable to the human, given they said 'ladder opens next canvas is OK in principle but must open earlier' — confirm the capstone's reward is now 'run the region' rather than 'reach the region.'

Father's name: confirm Kuranosuke vs an alternative (e.g. Sōbei, Heikurō); confirm he is the porter's-knot teacher (folding/refining the existing Denbei attribution) vs keeping Denbei as teacher and giving Kuranosuke a different signature.
How alive-but-ruined is too dark? Confirm the father's indenture/shame framing fits the 'they support his achievements' warm frame, or whether the human wants a less grim paternal arc.
Does the father's reveal change Oyuki's framing (currently a grieving widow)? If he's alive, her arc shifts from widowhood to a wife who let a comforting lie stand — confirm that re-read is wanted.
